element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Starting weapon; teaches basic combat timing and proximity awareness
Single enemies in open spaces; teaches aiming and threat recognition
Ranged alternative to knife; teaches ammunition management
Navigation gating; teaches exploration and key-hunting
Upgraded firepower; enables faster enemy elimination
Faster, more aggressive enemy variant; teaches reactive combat
Most powerful standard weapon; teaches resource scarcity
Unique difficult enemies requiring pattern recognition
Optional exploration mechanic; teaches environmental interaction
Resource management; tied to exploration and combat survival

typical_relief_triggers: Secret rooms with treasure and supplies
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast-paced action with natural breathing room in maze corridors; tension escalates toward level exits and boss encounters; relief provided by supply caches and secret discoveries

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Walls that 'slide back when triggered' indicate hidden passages; player learns to inspect walls for interactive tiles
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Activating certain wall tiles reveals secret rooms; convention established early and reinforced throughout

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with basic guards and simple layouts; knife and pistol sufficient for survival
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of dogs and submachine gun availability; maze complexity increases; key-hunting becomes central
difficulty_curve.late_game: Chain gun equipped enemies appear; boss encounters require pattern memorization; spatial layouts increasingly maze-like

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels open in relatively clear space allowing player to locate weapons and supplies before significant enemy contact
Interconnected corridors requiring key collection to reach elevator; enemy density increases in hub areas
Final level of each episode features singular high-difficulty enemy requiring sustained fire and movement
Hidden wall passages scattered throughout; optional engagement tied to player curiosity and reward incentives
